parts.text: I now have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile all findings.

I now have a complete picture of all booking-related functionality in this codebase. Here is the comprehensive summary. --- ## Comprehensive Summary: Booking/Call/Schedule Functionality ### Overview The project has a **fully integrated Cal.com booking feature** that is currently **active and wired into the UI**, but its actual runtime behavior depends entirely on environment variables that are not committed to the repository (`.env*` files are gitignored). --- ### 1. UI Components -- "Book a Call" / "Book a Consultation" Buttons **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/mgc-studio-web/components/hero-digital-success.tsx` This is the main (and only) page component. It contains **three** booking-related UI elements, all using the `` wrapper: #### (a) Mobile Header -- "Book a call" button (line 139-145) ```tsx Book a call ``` #### (b) Desktop Header -- "Book a call" button (line 175-181) ```tsx Book a call ``` #### (c) Main Hero CTA -- "Book a Consultation" button (line 196-206) ```tsx Book a Consultation ``` #### (d) Cal.com API Initialization (line 24-28) The component also initializes the Cal.com embed API at the top of the component: ```tsx useEffect(() => { (async function() { const cal = await getCalApi({ namespace: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_CAL_BOOK_A_CALL || '' }) cal('ui', { hideEventTypeDetails: false, layout: 'month_view' }) })() }, []) ``` --- ### 2. CalEmbed Wrapper Component **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/mgc-studio-web/components/cal-embed.tsx` A reusable wrapper component that renders a ` ) } export default CalEmbed ``` --- ### 3. Routes / Pages Related to Booking There are **no dedicated booking/scheduling routes or pages**. The entire app is a single-page application: **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/mgc-studio-web/app/page.tsx` ```tsx import { HeroDigitalSuccess } from '@/components/hero-digital-success'; export default function Home() { return (
); } ``` The booking experience is entirely handled by the Cal.com embed (a third-party popup/modal), not by custom routes. --- ### 4. Backend API Endpoints for Booking There are **no custom backend API endpoints** for booking. The project relies entirely on the Cal.com third-party service for scheduling logic. The `@calcom/embed-react` package communicates with Cal.com's servers directly. --- ### 5. Configuration / Dependencies #### Package dependency **File:** `/home/himmel/personal/mgc-studio-web/package.json` (line 13) ```json "@calcom/embed-react": "^1.5.3" ``` #### Required Environment Variables (not committed) Two environment variables are referenced but **no `.env` files exist in the repo** (they are gitignored via `.env*` in `.gitignore`): | Variable | Used In | Purpose | |---|---|---| | `NEXT_PUBLIC_CAL_BOOK_A_CALL` | `hero-digital-success.tsx` lines 26, 140, 176 | Cal.com link/namespace for "Book a Call" buttons (mobile + desktop header) | | `NEXT_PUBLIC_CAL_BOOK_CONSULTATION` | `hero-digital-success.tsx` line 197 | Cal.com link/namespace for "Book a Consultation" CTA | #### Cal.com embed configuration (hardcoded in `cal-embed.tsx`) ```json {"layout":"month_view","useSlotsViewOnSmallScreen":"true"} ``` --- ### 6. Feature Status Assessment | Aspect | Status | |---|---| | **UI Implementation** | COMPLETE -- Three booking buttons are rendered (mobile header, desktop header, hero CTA) | | **Cal.com Integration** | COMPLETE -- `@calcom/embed-react` package installed, `CalEmbed` component built, API initialized | | **Functional at Runtime** | CONDITIONAL -- Requires `NEXT_PUBLIC_CAL_BOOK_A_CALL` and `NEXT_PUBLIC_CAL_BOOK_CONSULTATION` env vars to be set. If empty strings, the `data-cal-link` attribute will be empty and the Cal.com popup will likely not open or show an error | | **Custom Backend** | NOT PRESENT -- All booking logic is delegated to Cal.com's hosted service | | **Dedicated Booking Page/Route** | NOT PRESENT -- No `/booking` or `/schedule` route exists | ### Summary The booking feature is **architecturally complete but externally dependent**. The codebase uses [Cal.com](https://cal.com) via the `@calcom/embed-react` SDK to provide two types of booking actions: a general "Book a call" (used in both mobile and desktop headers) and a "Book a Consultation" (used as the primary hero CTA). All booking UI is embedded into the single homepage via `` buttons that trigger Cal.com's popup calendar. The feature will only function when the two `NEXT_PUBLIC_CAL_*` environment variables are configured with valid Cal.com event type links/namespaces.
